seo.title: Building Content Generation with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation applications with OpenAI Assistants API enables powerful LLM-driven creation with built-in tools like code interpreter and file search. However, risks like hallucinated facts, brand voice drift, and context overflow can undermine quality and trust. Vex provides automated verification to enforce brand consistency, factual accuracy, and editorial standards at scale.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with a user request routed to a persistent Assistant configured with brand guidelines, style guides, and research files. The Assistant uses thread-based conversations with file search for reference documents and function calling for SEO checks or fact verification. On run completion, outputs are verified before publication.

Key components include the Assistant (pre-configured with instructions), Threads (conversation state), Runs (execution with tools), and Vector Stores (for brand documents). Webhooks capture run completion events for Vex verification, ensuring content meets quality thresholds before delivery.

This setup scales content creation while maintaining control through programmatic oversight and automated guardrails.

risks.name: Brand Voice Drift
risks.description: Assistant generates content deviating from established brand tone, vocabulary, or style guidelines
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic analysis against brand voice reference documents and style guides

risks.name: Factual Hallucinations
risks.description: Code interpreter or file search produces inaccurate information not grounded in source documents
risks.mitigation: Vex fact-checking against authoritative sources with citation verification

risks.name: Thread Context Overflow
risks.description: Conversation history exceeds token limits, causing silent truncation and inconsistent outputs
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token usage and triggers summarization or reset

risks.name: SEO Non-Compliance
risks.description: Generated content fails keyword density, readability scores, or technical SEO requirements
risks.mitigation: Vex automated SEO scoring with keyword optimization and readability checks

implementation.title: Create Content Generation Assistant
implementation.description: Configure Assistant with brand guidelines and research files for consistent content creation

implementation.title: Generate Content Thread
implementation.description: Create thread and run for topic-based content generation with progress monitoring
implementation.code:
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id,
    instructions=f"Write a 1200-word blog post about: {topic}"
)

# Poll for completion
while run.status != 'completed':
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id)
    time.sleep(1)

messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id)
content = messages.data[0].content[0].text.value

implementation.title: Setup Run Completion Webhook
implementation.description: Configure webhook to capture completed runs for Vex verification pipeline

implementation.title: TypeScript Content Generation
implementation.description: Equivalent TypeScript implementation for Node.js environments

vexIntegration.title: Brand Voice Verification Webhook
vexIntegration.description: Python webhook handler verifying brand consistency on run completion
vexIntegration.code:
from flask import Flask, request

import vex

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/vex-webhook', methods=['POST'])
def vex_webhook():
    event = request.json
    
    if event['event'] == 'thread.run.completed':
        thread_id = event['data']['thread_id']
        
        # Get content
        messages = openai.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
        content = messages.data[0].content[0].text.value
        
        # Vex brand verification
        result = vex.verify({
            'content': content,
            'policy': 'brand_voice',
            'reference_docs': ['brand_guidelines.pdf'],
            'threshold': 0.85
        })
        
        if result['passed']:
            publish(content)
        
    return 'OK', 200
